title: Confusion and fever in an 81-year-old female
presentation: An 81-year-old female with diabetes and CKD is brought in by her family for 2 days of confusion and decreased appetite. She has been incontinent and her family noticed she smells of urine. Vitals: BP 94/60, HR 118, RR 22, Temp 38.9C, SpO2 95%. Exam: dry mucous membranes, suprapubic tenderness, confusion, GCS 13.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Lactate >2 + hypotension + infection source = septic shock. Start IV fluids and antibiotics within 1 hour. Urosepsis is most common source in elderly females.
